questions.question: Why combine model checking and implementation tests in a lock-free capstone?
options: To connect abstract safety reasoning with concrete runtime behavior
questions.explanation: Model checks validate abstract transitions while implementation tests enforce practical behavior under concrete execution.

questions.question: What makes lock-free algorithms harder to verify than lock-based ones?
options: They use fewer lines of code
Concurrent interleavings and memory reordering create subtle state spaces
questions.explanation: Lock-free designs rely on atomic CAS operations whose correctness depends on every possible interleaving and memory model detail.

questions.question: What does a monotonic counter invariant assert?
options: Counter never decreases across valid transitions
questions.explanation: Monotonicity requires non-decreasing values through all permitted operations.

questions.question: What is a key limitation of a simplified TLA+ model for lock-free algorithms?
options: It may abstract away low-level memory-order details requiring implementation-level checks
questions.explanation: Abstract models can miss low-level details, so assumptions must be bridged with implementation checks and documentation.

questions.question: Why use property-based testing alongside TLA+ model checking?
options: Property tests catch implementation-level bugs that the abstract model doesn't cover
Property tests are faster to write than invariants
questions.explanation: TLA+ checks the abstract model; property-based testing on the actual implementation catches encoding bugs, memory-order issues, and API misuse.

_meta.title: Phase Capstone - Specify, Model-Check, and Prove a Lock-Free Algorithm
